title: Shiplap Fireplace TV Stand
summary: A freestanding 2x4-framed stand faced in shiplap, built with a cutout sized to a real electric fireplace insert — sized here for a 30"x24" insert but adjustable for 28–36" units.
description:
This is a movable furniture piece with a fireplace insert built in, not the floor-to-ceiling framed surround built elsewhere in this catalog — the whole thing sits on the floor as a low stand, sized to hold a TV on top and an electric insert in the middle. Build the front 2x4 framing first, spacing the inner studs to match your specific insert's rough-opening requirements, then check the frame for square by comparing opposite diagonal measurements before covering it.

Cover the front with shiplap, centering the insert opening on a horizontal shiplap joint so the finished look reads as balanced rather than accidental, then cut the opening out to fit your insert. **The 1x12 sides and top, added after the shiplap face, are what turn a bare stud frame into finished furniture** — attach the back framing next, which doubles as a wall-attachment point if you want the stand fixed in place rather than freestanding.

**This plan explicitly scales**: adjust the 2x4 cut lengths for a 28"–36" insert with only minor changes, and for a 40"+ insert the source notes the adjustment is still straightforward. Install your insert last, strictly by its own manufacturer instructions, after every piece of trim is on.

tools.note: For cutting out the insert opening.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut out for the insert opening; approximate quantity depends on insert size.

steps.title: Build the front framing
steps.body: Cut and build the front framing from the 2x4s with 2-1/2" screws, spacing the inner studs to match your insert's rough opening.

steps.title: Cover the front with shiplap
steps.body: Check the frame for square by comparing diagonal measurements. Cover the front with shiplap using glue and 1-1/4" brad nails, centering the insert opening on a horizontal joint, then cut out the opening for your insert.

steps.title: Attach the sides
steps.body: Attach the 1x12 sides with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails, adding screws if needed for extra security.

steps.title: Add the back framing
steps.body: Add the 2x4 back framing with screws. These boards can be used to attach the stand to the wall for a fixed install.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Measure and cut the top to fit, attaching with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails, with screws for extra security if needed.

steps.title: Add the trim
steps.body: Measure and cut the side trim, front trim, and front corner trim. Attach with glue and brad nails, adding screws to the front trim if needed.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Install the insert
steps.body: Follow the manufacturer's instructions to install the fireplace insert.

images.alt: Shiplap-faced fireplace TV stand with an electric insert
